title: podman-remote does not support most of the global flags
body:
podman-remote --help is showing a bunch of global flags that it
does not support

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago Your man page checker missed these global flags for podman-remote man page.
Man page checker does not test podman-remote.md. That may be a holdover from the varlink days; I'll take a look (tomorrow) and see what it'd take to enable.
@rhatdan Your fix is the correct method, you just need to filter through the sub-commands that are trying to now unnecessarily hide those flags.

Podman remote man page does not refer to these options.
@vrothberg you want me to change the flags to say that each global option does not effect the remote server on the regular podman.1.md?

> Podman remote man page does not refer to these options.
> @vrothberg you want me to change the flags to say that each global option does not effect the remote server on the regular podman.1.md?

I didn't check `podman-remote`. I think we're good as the options are not mentioned there.

Update: without a completely different set of man pages, or at least a robust well-established convention for marking options/subcommands in man pages as local-only, there's little hope for `hack/xref-helpmsgs-manpages` to check `podman-remote`. Even with a simple exclusion of `-l/--latest` I see 90 warnings, in both options (`run --authfile`, `rm -i`) and subcommands (`system migrate/renumber/reset`, `mount/umount/unshare`).
